name: Valdefuentes Trail
name_original: Sendero de Valdefuentes (SLC-VA 57) — circular desde Urueña
description: Jog the moderate 3.7-mile Valdefuentes Trail (SLC-VA 57) from Urueña, featuring diverse terrain and views of the Hermitage of Our Lady of th
region_intro: Jogging routes around Urueña are set within the Montes Torozos, a region characterized by its elevated position and panoramic views. The terrain includes pathways along a medieval wall, offering relatively flat or gently undulating surfaces. Surrounding natural landscapes feature accessible paths suitable for running, with some routes incorporating historical landmarks. Best jogging routes around Urueña The most popular jogging route is Embalse de la Espina — circular desde el Monasterio de la Santa Espina, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route features gentle elevation changes around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is Valdefuentes Trail, a moderate 3.7 miles (5.9 km) path. This route includes varied terrain with some elevation gain, offering views of the surrounding countryside. Local runners also love the Lavanda and Fuentes Route — circular trailrunning from Tiedra, a 9.7 miles (15.6 km) trail leading through open landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Jogging around Urueña is defined by elevated viewpoints, historical town walls, and accessible paths through natural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate runs to more challenging trail running. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 7 runners have used komoot to explore Urueña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of running terrain can I expect around Urueña?
A: Urueña, situated within the Montes Torozos, offers a diverse running experience. You'll find scenic pathways along the well-preserved medieval wall, providing relatively flat or gently undulating surfaces. Beyond the town, the natural landscapes feature accessible paths suitable for running, with some routes incorporating historical landmarks and offering panoramic views from its elevated position.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in the area?
A: While the guide primarily features moderate to difficult routes, many paths around Urueña are described as 'easily-accessible' and suitable for 'all skill levels' in the broader region. The elevated position of Urueña means some routes will have elevation changes, but options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119492853/embalse-de-la-espina-circular-desde-el-monasterio-de-la-santa-espina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Espina — circular desde el Monasterio de la Santa Espina&lt;/a&gt; are moderate with gentle elevation changes, making them suitable for those looking for a comfortable run.

Q: Can I find challenging trail running options near Urueña?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more challenging experience, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120246511/lavanda-and-fuentes-route-circular-trailrunning-from-tiedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavanda and Fuentes Route — circular trailrunning from Tiedra&lt;/a&gt; offer a longer distance of 9.7 miles (15.6 km) and are rated as difficult, providing a good test for experienced trail runners through open landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Absolutely. Many of the routes around Urueña are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119492853/embalse-de-la-espina-circular-desde-el-monasterio-de-la-santa-espina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Espina — circular desde el Monasterio de la Santa Espina&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120246511/lavanda-and-fuentes-route-circular-trailrunning-from-tiedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavanda and Fuentes Route — circular trailrunning from Tiedra&lt;/a&gt;, both designed as loops.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I see while running?
A: Urueña's rich history means you can incorporate several notable attractions into your runs. You might pass by the 11th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5709148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urueña Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the 12th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2473247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Annunciation&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3754394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of La Santa Espina&lt;/a&gt;. The town's medieval wall itself offers a unique historical backdrop for your run.

Q: Are there routes with good viewpoints for scenic running?
A: Yes, Urueña is known for its exceptional panoramic views due to its elevated position. Many routes offer stunning vistas of the surrounding countryside, including the Montes Torozos. On clear days, you might even see the Sierra de la Culebra and the Montes de León, providing a breathtaking backdrop for your run.

Q: What do other runners say about the routes in Urueña?
A: The routes around Urueña are appreciated by the komoot community for their varied terrain and scenic beauty. Runners often highlight the unique experience of jogging alongside the ancient town walls and the tranquility of the natural environment. Over 7 runners have used komoot to explore these trails, enjoying the mix of historical sights and open landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by water features?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119492853/embalse-de-la-espina-circular-desde-el-monasterio-de-la-santa-espina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Espina — circular desde el Monasterio de la Santa Espina&lt;/a&gt; route features gentle elevation changes around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bajoz River Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering pleasant views of the water during your run.

Q: What is the typical distance and elevation gain for running routes in this area?
A: The running routes around Urueña vary in length and elevation. You can find shorter runs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120232953/valdefuentes-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdefuentes Trail&lt;/a&gt; at 3.7 miles (5.9 km) with about 147 meters of elevation gain, up to longer routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120246511/lavanda-and-fuentes-route-circular-trailrunning-from-tiedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavanda and Fuentes Route&lt;/a&gt; at 9.7 miles (15.6 km) with around 113 meters of elevation. Most routes feature moderate elevation changes, making them enjoyable for various fitness levels.

Q: Is Urueña a good place for a peaceful and tranquil running experience?
A: Absolutely. Urueña is renowned for its peaceful atmosphere, offering a serene environment away from urban hustle. This tranquility significantly enhances the running experience, allowing for a more immersive connection with nature and the historical surroundings.

Q: Are there any lesser-known running loops worth exploring?
A: Beyond the most popular options, you might enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Mota del Marqués&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.3 km route, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of La Santa Espina loop from La Santa Espina&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.6 km. These offer a chance to explore different parts of the region while enjoying a good run.
tips:
The castle of Urueña, today only preserves the outer walls, having completely disappeared the different rooms that it could have had in the past.
It was ordered to be built around the year 1060 by the Castilian monarch Fernando I the Great on the remains of an ancient Roman fortification.

The Hermitage of Our Lady of the Annunciation is located in Urueña, in the province of Valladolid and is an outstanding example of Romanesque-Lombard art in Spain.
Built in the 12th century by order of the Infanta Sancha Raimúndez, on the site of a previous Mozarabic church. It is the only complete example of Romanesque-Lombard art in Castile and León.
The hermitage is located in a small valley a few kilometres from the town of Urueña, offering a magnificent view of the wall that surrounds the town.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog the Valdefuentes Trail?
faqs.answer: The Valdefuentes Trail is approximately 5.9 kilometers (3.7 miles) long. For jogging, it typically takes around 45-50 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is the Valdefuentes Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, meaning you will start and end in the same location in Urueña.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for the Valdefuentes Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail begins and ends in the historic walled town of Urueña, located in the province of Valladolid, Spain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Valdefuentes Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain. You'll experience a descent into the Arroyo de la Ermita valley, followed by a gradual, comfortable ascent through cultivated fields and sparse holm oaks. The route then flattens out on the calcareous plateau of Teso de San Cristóbal for about two kilometers. Be prepared for a more challenging final 600-meter ascent with an 11% gradient to return to Urueña.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy diverse landscapes, including views of Urueña from the Teso de San Cristóbal plateau. There's a charming section among poplar and pine trees, and you'll pass by the Romanesque Hermitage of Our Lady of the Annunciation. The walled town of Urueña itself provides a picturesque backdrop.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The trail offers magnificent views of Urueña's walls from the valley where the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Annunciation is located. You'll also get panoramic views from the Teso de San Cristóbal plateau. The starting point, Urueña Castle, is another significant landmark.

faqs.question: What should I know about the difficulty of this trail for jogging?
faqs.answer: While much of the trail features comfortable, gradual ascents and flat sections, it is rated as moderate due to a specific challenge: a final 600-meter climb with an 11% gradient. This section requires a good level of fitness for jogging.

faqs.question: Is the Valdefuentes Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through cultivated fields or near livestock, and to carry water for them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog the Valdefuentes Trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for jogging this trail, offering milder temperatures and beautiful natural colors. Summers can be hot, so if you go then, an early morning or late afternoon start is advisable. Winters can be chilly, but often clear.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and jog the Valdefuentes Trail (SLC-VA 57).

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Valdefuentes Trail?
faqs.answer: Since the trail starts and ends in Urueña, you can typically find parking within the town. Urueña is a small, historic town, and parking options are usually available near the town's entrance or main areas.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of the Annunciation
